title: leucanthemum ircutianum subsp. ircutianum
intro: Leucanthemum ircutianum subsp. ircutianum, commonly known as the Russian coneflower, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Eastern Siberia. The Russian coneflower has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Leucanthemum ircutianum subsp. ircutianum can be prepared as an herbal infusion, tincture, or decoction, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms of traditional medicine.
